DISCOVERY, 612, PRESENT: HAROLD R. JONES, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANTS/MOVING PARTIES, UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA AT SAN FRANCISCO, ET AL. CHARLOTTE E. SCOTT, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF/OPPOSING PARTY. HEARING HELD. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING LEAVE TO COMPLETE PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE MEDICAL EVALUATION IS NOT GOOD CAUSE FOR FURTHER DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF. AS TO THE REQUEST FOR FURTHER DEPOSITION ON LATER PRODUCED DOCUMENTS, THE COURT FINDS THAT THE REQUEST WAS NOT MADE WITH DILIGENCE AS AN UNDUE AMOUNT OF TIME WAS ALLOWED TO ELAPSE WITHOUT SEEKING TO DEPOSE PLAINTIFF FURTHER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

DISCOVERY 612, APPEARANCES: CHARLOTTE SCOTT, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY. HAROLD R. JONES, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY. MOTION ARGUED. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AND MODIFIED TO ADD REQUESTS FOR ACCOMODATION MADE TO EMPLOYER. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO QUASH MEDICAL AND EMPLOYMENT RECORDS SUBPOENAS IS GRANTED AS THE SUBPOENAS ARE OVERLY BROAD. ANY SUBPOENAS ISSUED MUST BE LIMITED TO THE TREATMENT AND MEDICAL CONDITIONS RELATED TO PLAINTIFF'S DISABILITY AND CLAIMS IN THIS LITIGATION. AS TO EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, THE SUBPOENA MUST BE LIMITED TO TREATMENT FOR THE EMOTIONAL DISTRESS ARISING OUT OF THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE COMPLAINT. AS TO OTHER MEDICAL SUBPOENAS, THEY MUST BE LIMITED TO TREATMENT OF THE CONDITIONS THAT LED TO THE REQUEST FOR ACCOMMODATION. AS TO THE EMPLOYMENT RECORDS, THE SUBPOENAS MUST BE LIMITED TO DOCUMENTS REFLECTING PLAINTIFF'S RESIGNATION AND/OR TERMINATION, THE EMPLOYMENT APPLICATION, AND DOCUMENTS REGARDING JOB DUTIES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
ORDER GRANTING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO QUASH MEDICAL RECORDS AND EMPLOYMENT RECORDS SUBPOENAS

ORDER GRANTING EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ORDER SHORTENING TIME TO HEAR PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT'S FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, EMPLOYMENT FORM INTERROGATORIES; VERIFICATIONS TO WRITTEN DISCOVERY; PRIVILEGE LOG

DISCOVERY 612, APPEARANCES: CLINT BRAYTON, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY, JACQUELYN WADE. FORREST E. FANG, ESQ. AND HAROLD R. JONES, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY, UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA AT SAN FRANCISCO. MATTER ARGUED. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT'S FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, VERIFICATIONS TO WRITTEN DISCOVERY, AND PRIVILEGE LOG IS MOOT IN PART, GRANTED IN PART, AND DENIED IN PART. AS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NOS. 7, 8, 21, 22, 26, 27, 31, 32, 33, AND 38, THEY ARE MOOT AS DEFENDANT ASSERTS THAT THE ISSUES HAVE BEEN RESOLVED. AS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NOS. 29, 34, 35, AND 36, THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED EXCEPT FOR CLAIMS OF PRIVILEGE. IF ANY DOCUMENTS HAVE BEEN WITHHELD PURSUANT TO A CLAIM OF PRIVILEGE, THEY MUST BE IDENTIFIED IN A PRIVILEGE LOG. DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO RESPOND FURTHER, LIMITED TO THE DATE OF ONE YEAR FROM THE DATE OF PLAINTIFF'S SEPARATION. AS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NO. 32, THE RESPONSE THAT DISCOVERY IS CONTINUING IS IMPROPER. A RESPONSE MUST BE COMPLETE AT THE TIME IT IS MADE. THERE IS NO STATUTORY AUTHORITY THAT ALLOWS A RESERVATION OF A RIGHT TO PRODUCE ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS. AS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NOS. 37, 39, AND 40, RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS SHALL BE PRODUCED WITH NAMES AND IDENTIFYING INFORMATION REDACTED EXCEPT AS FORMAL COMPLAINTS WERE MADE. THIS ORDER DOES NOT INCLUDE COMPLAINTS THAT ARE NOT MAINTAINED IN DOCUMENTS ONLY IN INDIVIDUAL PERSONNEL RECORDS AS THERE HAS BEEN NO WAIVER OF PRIVACY. FURTHER RESPONSES SHALL BE MADE WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ADDITIONAL ORDERS: AS TO DOCUMENT REQUEST NOS. 34, 35, AND 36, PRODUCTION IS LIMITED TO DOCUMENTS RELATING TO JOBS IDENTIFIED BY PLAINTIFF. AS TO NOS. 37 AND 39 -- LIMITED TO THE PERIOD 2002 AND 2003. AS TO NO. 40 -- DEFENDANT SHALL REDACT NAMES OF PERSONS WHO MADE COMPLAINTS AND LIMITED TO THE YEARS 2002 AND 2003. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

LAW AND MOTION 301, APPEARANCES:FOR DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY & FOR PLAINTIFF AND OPPOSING PARTY: ARGUED; DEFENDANT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT:& 1. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION RE DISABILITY DISCRIMINATION UNDER FEHA - DENIED; TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT RE QUALIFICATION AND NO STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS DEFENSE. ADMINISTRATIVE SEPARATION ON 11/02 AND ADMINISTRATIVE COMPLAINT FILED 07/03. 2. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FOR RETALIATION - DENIED; TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WHETHER DEFENDANT MEDICALLY SEPARATED PLAINTIFF TO RETALIATE FOR HER REQUEST FOR ACCOMODATION. 3. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FOR TERMINATION IN BREACH OF PUBLIC POLICY - GRANTED. 4. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FOR 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICES - GRANTED. 5. NO SECTION CCP 437C(H) CONTINUANCE. 6. COURT WILL NOT CONSIDER NEW EVIDENCE PRESENTED IN THE REPLY. ORDER SIGNED. (DEPT. 301 JUDGE A. JAMES ROBERTSON 11 REPORTER: JOYCE LONG, CSR #8620

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Forrest E. Fang, Esq. and Harold Jones, Esq., Lafayette & Kumagai (415) 357-4600 for Defendant and Moving Party University of California at San Francisco and Regents Of The University of California
Clint J. Brayton, Esq., Schneider & Wallace (415) 421-7100 for Plaintiff and Opposing Party Wade
RULING:
A R G U E D; the Court adopted its Tentative Ruling: DEFENDANT UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA AT SAN FRANCISCO AND REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT:
1. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION RE DISABILITY DISCRIMINATION UNDER FEHA - DENIED; TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT RE QUALIFICATION AND NO STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS DEFENSE. ADMINISTRATIVE SEPARATION ON 11/02 AND ADMINISTRATIVE COMPLAINT FILED 07/03.
2. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FOR RETALIATION - DENIED; TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WHETHER DEFENDANT MEDICALLY SEPARATED PLAINTIFF TO RETALIATE FOR HER REQUEST FOR ACCOMODATION.
3. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FOR TERMINATION IN BREACH OF PUBLIC POLICY - GRANTED.
4. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FOR 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICES - GRANTED.
5. NO SECTION CCP 437c(h) CONTINUANCE.
6. COURT WILL NOT CONSIDER NEW EVIDENCE PRESENTED IN THE REPLY. Order signed. (301/AJR/AC)
Reporter: Joyce Long, CSR #8620
